   688→#### `frontend/src/__tests__/product-carousel.test.tsx` — ProductCarousel component
   689→
   690→```typescript
   691→import { describe, it, expect, vi } from "vitest";
   692→import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/react";
   693→import ProductCarousel from "@/components/mirror/ProductCarousel";
   694→
   695→const mockItems = [
   696→  {
   697→    product_id: "p1",
   698→    title: "Nike Air Max 90",
   699→    price: "$129.99",
   700→    image_url: "https://example.com/shoe.jpg",
   701→    link: "https://example.com/shoe",
   702→    source: "Nordstrom",
   703→  },
   704→  {
   705→    product_id: "p2",
   706→    title: "Zara Oversized Blazer",
   707→    price: "$89.00",
   708→    image_url: "https://example.com/blazer.jpg",
   709→    link: "https://example.com/blazer",
   710→    source: "Zara",
   711→  },
   712→];
   713→
   714→describe("ProductCarousel", () => {
   715→  it("renders nothing when items array is empty", () => {
   716→    const { container } = render(
   717→      <ProductCarousel items={[]} onGesture={vi.fn()} />
   718→    );
   719→    expect(container.firstChild).toBeNull();
   720→  });
   721→
   722→  it("renders product cards with title and price", () => {
   723→    render(<ProductCarousel items={mockItems} onGesture={vi.fn()} />);
   724→
   725→    expect(screen.getByText("Nike Air Max 90")).toBeDefined();
   726→    expect(screen.getByText("$129.99")).toBeDefined();
   727→    expect(screen.getByText("Zara Oversized Blazer")).toBeDefined();
   728→  });
   729→
   730→  it("renders source labels on cards", () => {
   731→    render(<ProductCarousel items={mockItems} onGesture={vi.fn()} />);
   732→
   733→    expect(screen.getByText("Nordstrom")).toBeDefined();
   734→    expect(screen.getByText("Zara")).toBeDefined();
   735→  });
   736→
   737→  it("renders product images with correct src", () => {
   738→    render(<ProductCarousel items={mockItems} onGesture={vi.fn()} />);
   739→
   740→    const images = screen.getAllByRole("img");
   741→    expect(images).toHaveLength(2);
   742→    expect(images[0].getAttribute("src")).toContain("shoe.jpg");
   743→  });
   744→
   745→  it("calls onGesture when gesture event is received", () => {
   746→    const onGesture = vi.fn();
   747→    render(<ProductCarousel items={mockItems} onGesture={onGesture} />);
   748→
   749→    // Simulate a swipe_right on the first item
   750→    onGesture("swipe_right", mockItems[0]);
   751→    expect(onGesture).toHaveBeenCalledWith("swipe_right", mockItems[0]);
   752→  });
   753→
   754→  it("replaces items when new items prop arrives", () => {
   755→    const { rerender } = render(
   756→      <ProductCarousel items={mockItems} onGesture={vi.fn()} />
   757→    );
   758→
   759→    const newItems = [
   760→      { ...mockItems[0], title: "Adidas Ultraboost", product_id: "p3" },
   761→    ];
   762→    rerender(<ProductCarousel items={newItems} onGesture={vi.fn()} />);
   763→
   764→    expect(screen.getByText("Adidas Ultraboost")).toBeDefined();
   765→    expect(screen.queryByText("Nike Air Max 90")).toBeNull();
   766→  });
   767→});
   768→```

1. The full API surface: initialization, session management, speaking/TTS, events, cleanup
2. The token flow: how to create session tokens, start sessions, what LiveKit returns
3. What events/callbacks are available (equivalent to StreamingEvents.STREAM_READY, AVATAR_START_TALKING, AVATAR_STOP_TALKING, STREAM_DISCONNECTED)
4. The `speak()` method signature — does it support TaskType.REPEAT?
5. How to get the video/media stream for rendering

Fetch these URLs for information:
- https://docs.liveavatar.com/docs/quick-start-guide
- https://docs.liveavatar.com/docs/configuring-full-mode
- https://docs.liveavatar.com/reference/create-session-token
- https://docs.liveavatar.com/reference/start-session
- https://github.com/heygen-com/liveavatar-web-sdk

Also search npm for `@heygen/liveavatar-web-sdk` to understand the package exports and version.

content.content:
1→"""HeyGen streaming avatar token endpoint."""
     2→
     3→import os
     4→
     5→import httpx
     6→from fastapi import APIRouter, HTTPException
     7→
     8→router = APIRouter(prefix="/api/heygen", tags=["heygen"])
     9→
    10→
    11→@router.get("/token")
    12→async def get_heygen_token():
    13→    """Generate a short-lived HeyGen session token for the streaming avatar SDK."""
    14→    api_key = os.environ.get("HEYGEN_API_KEY")
    15→    if not api_key:
    16→        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail="HEYGEN_API_KEY not configured")
    17→
    18→    async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client:
    19→        resp = await client.post(
    20→            "https://api.heygen.com/v1/streaming.create_token",
    21→            headers={"x-api-key": api_key},
    22→        )
    23→
    24→    if resp.status_code != 200:
    25→        raise HTTPException(status_code=502, detail=f"HeyGen API error: {resp.text}")
    26→
    27→    return {"token": resp.json()["data"]["token"]}
    28→

1→"""Tests for the HeyGen token endpoint."""
     2→
     3→import pytest
     4→from unittest.mock import patch, AsyncMock, MagicMock
     5→from httpx import AsyncClient, ASGITransport
     6→from main import app
     7→
     8→transport = ASGITransport(app=app)
     9→
    10→
    11→@pytest.mark.asyncio
    12→async def test_get_heygen_token_success():
    13→    """GET /api/heygen/token returns a session token from HeyGen API."""
    14→    mock_response = MagicMock()
    15→    mock_response.status_code = 200
    16→    mock_response.json.return_value = {"data": {"token": "hg_test_token_123"}}
    17→
    18→    with patch("routers.heygen.httpx.AsyncClient") as MockClient:
    19→        instance = AsyncMock()
    20→        instance.post.return_value = mock_response
    21→        instance.__aenter__ = AsyncMock(return_value=instance)
    22→        instance.__aexit__ = AsyncMock(return_value=False)
    23→        MockClient.return_value = instance
    24→
    25→        async with AsyncClient(transport=transport, base_url="http://test") as ac:
    26→            response = await ac.get("/api/heygen/token")
    27→
    28→    assert response.status_code == 200
    29→    assert response.json()["token"] == "hg_test_token_123"
    30→
    31→
    32→@pytest.mark.asyncio
    33→async def test_get_heygen_token_missing_api_key():
    34→    """Returns 500 when HEYGEN_API_KEY is not set."""
    35→    with patch("routers.heygen.os.environ.get", return_value=None):
    36→        async with AsyncClient(transport=transport, base_url="http://test") as ac:
    37→            response = await ac.get("/api/heygen/token")
    38→
    39→    assert response.status_code == 500
    40→
    41→
    42→@pytest.mark.asyncio
    43→async def test_get_heygen_token_heygen_api_error():
    44→    """Returns 502 when HeyGen API returns an error."""
    45→    mock_response = MagicMock()
    46→    mock_response.status_code = 401
    47→    mock_response.text = "Unauthorized"
    48→
    49→    with patch("routers.heygen.httpx.AsyncClient") as MockClient:
    50→        instance = AsyncMock()
    51→        instance.post.return_value = mock_response
    52→        instance.__aenter__ = AsyncMock(return_value=instance)
    53→        instance.__aexit__ = AsyncMock(return_value=False)
    54→        MockClient.return_value = instance
    55→
    56→        async with AsyncClient(transport=transport, base_url="http://test") as ac:
    57→            response = await ac.get("/api/heygen/token")
    58→
    59→    assert response.status_code == 502
    60→

data.prompt:
Analyze the current HeyGen avatar integration in the mirrorless project to understand everything that needs to change for a LiveAvatar migration. Specifically:

1. Read `frontend/src/hooks/useHeyGenAvatar.ts` — document every method, event, state variable, and the speech queue mechanism
2. Read `frontend/src/components/mirror/AvatarPiP.tsx` — how does it render the avatar video?
3. Read `frontend/src/app/mirror/page.tsx` — how is `useHeyGenAvatar` consumed? What properties are used?
4. Read `backend/routers/heygen.py` — the current token endpoint
5. Read `frontend/src/__tests__/` — are there any avatar-related tests?
6. Check `frontend/package.json` for the current `@heygen/streaming-avatar` version
7. Search for any other files that import from `@heygen/streaming-avatar` or reference HeyGen

I need a complete inventory of what touches the HeyGen integration so nothing is missed in the migration.

I found relevant documentation for the @heygen/streaming-avatar SDK. Here's information about events, the speak method, and TaskType:

## TaskType

TaskType has two main values: TALK (avatar will talk in response to text sent, with responses provided by HeyGen's connection to GPT-4o mini) and REPEAT (avatar will simply repeat the text sent).

## speak() Method

The speak method is used to make the avatar speak, with parameters like text and task_type (e.g., `await speak({ text: "Hello, world!", task_type: TaskType.REPEAT })`).

## Events

The SDK emits a variety of events during a streaming session, which can be captured to update the UI or trigger additional logic. The main events include:

- AVATAR_START_TALKING: Emitted when the avatar starts speaking
- AVATAR_STOP_TALKING: Emitted when the avatar stops speaking
- AVATAR_TALKING_MESSAGE: Triggered when the avatar sends a speaking message
- AVATAR_END_MESSAGE: Triggered when the avatar finishes sending messages
- USER_TALKING_MESSAGE: Emitted when the user sends a speaking message
- USER_END_MESSAGE: Triggered when the user finishes sending messages
- STREAM_READY: Indicates that the stream is ready for display
- STREAM_DISCONNECTED: Triggered when the stream disconnects

You can register event listeners using the `on()` method (e.g., `on('messageReceived', (message) => { ... }))`).

I found several relevant results for your search on "streaming-avatar" site:github.com HeyGen. Here are the main findings:

## Official HeyGen Repositories

HeyGen has released an SDK as an NPM package that developers can add to their websites for Streaming Avatar functionality. The main repositories include:

1. **StreamingAvatar** - A HeyGen Streaming Avatar demo that serves as a starting point for developers to adapt and build streaming sessions into their own websites and experiences.

2. **StreamingAvatarSDK** - This SDK provides a convenient way for developers to better interface with HeyGen's Interactive Avatar.

3. **StreamingAvatarTSDemo** - A common use case for a Streaming Avatar is to use it as the 'face' of an LLM that users can interact with, including functionality to accept user input via voice using OpenAI's Whisper library and send that input to an OpenAI LLM model.

## Avatar Setup Requirements

Either an API Key or Trial Token from HeyGen is required to run the Streaming API demo, with API Keys reserved for Enterprise customers, whereas both Creator and Teams plan users can activate and use a Trial token.

## Community Projects

There are also several community-created projects built on HeyGen's Streaming API, including React Native implementations and Next.js applications showcasing various integration approaches.

content.content:
# Streaming Avatar SDK API Reference Summary

## Core Class
The **`StreamingAvatar`** class manages avatar streaming sessions, initialization, and real-time communication. Initialize it with: `new StreamingAvatar({ token: "access-token" })`

## Key Interfaces

**StreamingAvatarApiConfig**: Configuration requiring an authentication token (obtained from the create_token endpoint, not your API key) and optional base path.

**StartAvatarRequest**: Configures sessions with avatar ID, quality level, voice settings, knowledge base, speech-to-text parameters, language, and timeout behavior.

**StartAvatarResponse**: Returns session metadata including session ID, access token, WebSocket URL, and duration limits.

## Essential Methods

- **`newSession()`**: Creates and starts a new session
- **`speak()`**: Sends text for avatar vocalization
- **`startVoiceChat()` / `closeVoiceChat()`**: Manages voice interaction
- **`startListening()` / `stopListening()`**: Controls audio input
- **`interrupt()`**: Halts current speaking
- **`stopAvatar()`**: Terminates session

## Task Types

- **TALK**: Avatar generates responses via GPT-4o mini integration
- **REPEAT**: Avatar vocalizes provided text directly

## Event Handling

Register listeners via `on()` and remove via `off()`. Key events:
- Avatar states: `AVATAR_START_TALKING`, `AVATAR_STOP_TALKING`
- Stream status: `STREAM_READY`, `STREAM_DISCONNECTED`
- User interaction: `USER_START`, `USER_STOP`, `USER_SILENCE`

## Quality & Voice Options

**AvatarQuality**: High (720p/2000kbps), Medium (480p/1000kbps), Low (360p/500kbps)

**VoiceEmotion**: EXCITED, SERIOUS, FRIENDLY, SOOTHING, BROADCASTER

Based on the search results, here's what I found about HeyGen's `create_token` streaming avatar API endpoint:

The create_token endpoint is used to generate a new access token for a streaming session.

## Endpoint Details

**URL:** `https://api.heygen.com/v1/streaming.create_token`

**Method:** POST

**Authentication:** The endpoint requires an `x-api-key` header

## Usage Example

Here's how to fetch an access token using the endpoint:
```
const response = await fetch(
  "https://api.heygen.com/v1/streaming.create_token",
  {
    method: "POST",
    headers: { "x-api-key": apiKey },
  }
);
const { data } = await response.json();
return data.token;
```


## Response Format

The response includes a `data` object containing a `token` field, which you can then use to initialize the StreamingAvatar SDK with your access token.

This token is essential for establishing authenticated streaming sessions with HeyGen's interactive avatars.

Based on the search results, I found information about the HeyGen Streaming Avatar SDK's `speak` method and related methods:

## `speak` Method

The `speak` method sends a command to the avatar to speak the provided text, with additional parameters like `task_type` allowing for more advanced control, like repeating or talking.

### Method Signature and Parameters

The `speak` method can be called with parameters including `text`, `task_type` (such as `TaskType.REPEAT`), and `taskMode` (such as `TaskMode.SYNC`).

A basic example usage:
The method is called as `await avatar.speak({ text: 'Hello!' })` with error handling available.

### Task Types

The `TALK` task type makes the avatar talk in response to text, using HeyGen's connection to GPT-4o mini influenced by provided KnowledgeID or KnowledgeBase. The `REPEAT` task type makes the avatar simply repeat the provided text.

## `createStartAvatar` Method

The `createStartAvatar` method creates and starts a new session using the provided StartAvatarRequest data, returning detailed session information such as the session ID and other metadata.

Example parameters for `createStartAvatar` include `avatarName`, `quality` (set to 'high'), and voice settings like `voiceId`.

Perfect! Now I have everything I need. Let me compile a comprehensive inventory report.

## HeyGen Avatar Integration Analysis - Complete Inventory

Based on my thorough code review, here's a complete inventory of everything that touches the HeyGen integration in the mirrorless project:

---

### **1. Frontend Hook: `useHeyGenAvatar`**
**File:** `/Users/johnathanmo/mirrorless/frontend/src/hooks/useHeyGenAvatar.ts`

**Methods & Public API:**
- `startAvatar()` — Fetches token, creates StreamingAvatar instance, sets up event listeners
- `stopAvatar()` — Cleans up avatar session, clears queue, resets refs
- `speak(text)` — Queues text and processes speech queue
- `interrupt()` — Stops current speech and clears queue
- `clearQueue()` — Manually flushes speech queue

**State Variables:**
- `isReady` (boolean) — Avatar stream is connected and ready
- `isSpeaking` (boolean) — Avatar is currently speaking
- `videoRef` (RefObject) — Attached to `<video>` element showing avatar stream
- `avatarRef` (internal) — Reference to StreamingAvatar SDK instance
- `speechQueueRef` (internal array) — Queued text awaiting speech
- `isProcessingQueueRef` (internal boolean) — Guard flag for queue processing

**Events Listened:**
- `StreamingEvents.STREAM_READY` — Attaches MediaStream to videoRef
- `StreamingEvents.AVATAR_START_TALKING` — Sets isSpeaking=true
- `StreamingEvents.AVATAR_STOP_TALKING` — Resets queue processor, triggers next sentence
- `StreamingEvents.STREAM_DISCONNECTED` — Cleanup, resets ready state

**Speech Queue Mechanism:**
- One-at-a-time processing to prevent HeyGen garbling
- `processQueue()` shifts next item from queue, calls `avatar.speak({ text, taskType: TaskType.REPEAT })`
- Processing guard (`isProcessingQueueRef`) prevents concurrent calls
- Queue auto-advances on `AVATAR_STOP_TALKING` event
- If `speak()` fails, resets processing flag and retries queue

**Configuration:**
- Avatar quality: `AvatarQuality.Medium`
- Avatar name: `AVATAR_ID` (env var `NEXT_PUBLIC_HEYGEN_AVATAR_ID` or default)
- Token endpoint: `GET {API_URL}/api/heygen/token`

**Import Chain:**
```typescript
import StreamingAvatar, { AvatarQuality, StreamingEvents, TaskType } from "@heygen/streaming-avatar"
```

---

### **2. Frontend Component: `AvatarPiP`**
**File:** `/Users/johnathanmo/mirrorless/frontend/src/components/mirror/AvatarPiP.tsx`

**Props:**
- `videoRef` (RefObject<HTMLVideoElement>) — Passed from useHeyGenAvatar
- `isReady` (boolean) — Controls visibility/loading state

**Rendering:**
- Absolute positioned: top-right (top: 20px, right: 20px)
- Size: 15vw width, 1:1 aspect ratio, min 160px, max 240px
- Styled: 16px border radius, 2px white border, blue glow shadow
- `<video>` element with autoPlay, playsInline, object-fit: cover
- Hidden when `!isReady`, shows "Loading avatar..." placeholder
- z-index: 10 (below mirror text, above webcam)

**Note:** Pure presentation component, no logic.

---

### **3. Mirror Page Integration**
**File:** `/Users/johnathanmo/mirrorless/frontend/src/app/mirror/page.tsx`

**Hook Usage:**
```typescript
const avatar = useHeyGenAvatar();
```

**Properties Consumed:**
- `avatar.videoRef` — Passed to AvatarPiP component
- `avatar.isReady` — Passed to AvatarPiP, checked before showing PiP
- `avatar.isSpeaking` — Used to queue/defer voice transcripts from Deepgram
- `avatar.startAvatar()` — Called on `session_active` event and fallback speech detection
- `avatar.stopAvatar()` — Called on `session_ended` event
- `avatar.speak(text)` — Called via SentenceBuffer when `mira_speech` chunks arrive
- `avatar.clearQueue()` — Called on session end and error

**Socket.io Integration Points:**
- **Outbound:** None (avatar is passive, speaks when told)
- **Inbound events that trigger avatar:**
  - `mira_speech` — Text chunks → sentenceBuffer → avatar.speak()
  - `session_active` — Triggers avatar.startAvatar()
  - `session_ended` — Triggers avatar.stopAvatar()

**Session Lifecycle:**
1. On page load: avatar NOT started
2. `session_active` event → `avatar.startAvatar()` (2-3s initialization)
3. During session: `mira_speech` chunks streamed to avatar
4. `session_ended` → `avatar.stopAvatar()` + cleanup

**Conditional Rendering:**
```typescript
{sessionActive && (
  <AvatarPiP videoRef={avatar.videoRef} isReady={avatar.isReady} />
)}
```

---

### **4. Backend Token Endpoint**
**File:** `/Users/johnathanmo/mirrorless/backend/routers/heygen.py`

**Endpoint:**
- `GET /api/heygen/token`

**Implementation:**
- Reads `HEYGEN_API_KEY` from environment
- POSTs to `https://api.heygen.com/v1/streaming.create_token` with `x-api-key` header
- Returns `{"token": resp.json()["data"]["token"]}`
- Error handling: 500 if key missing, 502 if HeyGen API fails

**Dependency:** `httpx.AsyncClient` for async HTTP requests

**Registration in main.py:**
```python
from routers import auth, queue, users, heygen
app.include_router(heygen.router)
```

---

### **5. Package Dependencies**
**File:** `/Users/johnathanmo/mirrorless/frontend/package.json`

**Current Version:**
```json
"@heygen/streaming-avatar": "^2.1.0"
```

**Related Dependencies (used together):**
- `livekit-client: ^2.17.1` — WebRTC infrastructure (may be needed for alternative avatar APIs)
- `socket.io-client: ^4.8.0` — Real-time communication

---

### **6. Tests**
**File:** `/Users/johnathanmo/mirrorless/backend/tests/test_heygen_routes.py`

**Tests:**
1. `test_get_heygen_token_success()` — 200 response with token
2. `test_get_heygen_token_missing_api_key()` — 500 when env var missing
3. `test_get_heygen_token_heygen_api_error()` — 502 when HeyGen API fails

**No frontend unit tests** for useHeyGenAvatar (integration tests only via page tests)

---

### **7. Environment Variables**
**Backend `.env`:**
```
HEYGEN_API_KEY=<actual-key>
```

**Frontend `.env.local`:**
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_HEYGEN_AVATAR_ID` (optional, defaults to "default")
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` (used to fetch token from backend)

---

### **8. Documentation References**
**Files mentioning HeyGen:**
- `/Users/johnathanmo/mirrorless/CLAUDE.md` — Architecture overview
- `/Users/johnathanmo/mirrorless/docs/plans/2026-02-14-mirror-wiring-spec.md` — Complete implementation spec with lifecycle, error cases, socket events
- `/Users/johnathanmo/mirrorless/.env.example` — HEYGEN_API_KEY placeholder
- `SPEC.md` — High-level project spec
- Research docs in `/docs/plans/` and `/docs/task3-*`

---

### **9. Related Systems (Not Directly HeyGen)**
These systems feed text TO the avatar but aren't HeyGen-specific:

**SentenceBuffer** (`frontend/src/lib/sentence-buffer.ts`):
- Chunks `mira_speech` → sentences → calls callback with `avatar.speak()`
- Detects sentence boundaries (. ! ?) to avoid fragmented speech

**Deepgram STT** (`frontend/src/hooks/useDeepgramSTT.ts`):
- Captures user voice input
- Transcripts held while avatar.isSpeaking (prevents interrupt)
- Flushed when avatar stops

**Socket.io** (Backend → Frontend):
- `mira_speech` event carries avatar text
- Session lifecycle events trigger avatar start/stop

---

### **10. Imports Search Results**
Only 2 files import HeyGen SDK directly:
1. `frontend/src/hooks/useHeyGenAvatar.ts` — Imports SDK + types
2. `frontend/src/app/mirror/page.tsx` — Imports hook

No other files touch HeyGen internals.

---

## **Summary: What Changes for LiveAvatar Migration**

### **Must Replace:**
1. ✅ `useHeyGenAvatar.ts` — Entire hook (different SDK API)
2. ✅ `routers/heygen.py` — Token endpoint URL + request/response format
3. ✅ `package.json` — Remove `@heygen/streaming-avatar`, add HeyGen LiveAvatar package

### **May Need Updates:**
4. ⚠️ `mirror/page.tsx` — Lifecycle methods may change (check token fetch URL)
5. ⚠️ `AvatarPiP.tsx` — If HeyGen LiveAvatar uses different video stream attachment
6. ⚠️ Test file — If token endpoint response format changes

### **No Changes Needed:**
- ✅ SentenceBuffer (receives text, format unchanged)
- ✅ Deepgram integration (voice input unchanged)
- ✅ Socket.io events (all text/session events unchanged)
- ✅ Mirror page logic (just swaps hook, same interface)
- ✅ Environment variables (just values change, not names)

---

**Next Step:** Review HeyGen's LiveAvatar SDK documentation to confirm:
1. Token endpoint URL and authentication method
2. SDK class/method names and event signatures
3. Video stream attachment pattern (same as Streaming Avatar?)
4. Any new quality/configuration options

content.content:
# HeyGen Streaming Avatar TypeScript Demo

This repository demonstrates integrating HeyGen's Streaming Avatar SDK with React and TypeScript, though **it's now deprecated** in favor of their NextJS starter project.

## Key Setup Requirements

To run this demo, you need:

1. **HeyGen API Credentials**: Obtain either an Enterprise API Token or Trial Token from [app.heygen.com/settings?nav=API](https://app.heygen.com/settings?nav=API)
2. **Environment Configuration**: Place your token in the `Server/.env` file
3. **Node.js & npm**: Install dependencies via `npm install`

## Trial vs. Enterprise Tokens

The Trial Token (available to all users) limits you to "3 concurrent streaming sessions" but charges nothing per session. Sessions auto-close after 10 minutes of inactivity. Opening more than 3 simultaneously causes "stuttering and freezing."

## Core Functionality

**Starting Sessions**: Click 'start' to initialize a Streaming Avatar. Text entered in the input field gets spoken when you click 'Speak'.

**Avatar Selection**: You can swap avatars by entering different IDs before starting a session. Only Finetune Instant and Studio Avatars support streaming; Photo Avatars are incompatible.

**Voice Configuration**: Most HeyGen AI voices work with Streaming. Some voices (Christine, Tarquin) support emotions via the Voice Setting parameter, and you can adjust speech rate.

## OpenAI Integration (Optional)

The demo connects to OpenAI's Chat Completions API to make avatars respond intelligently. You customize the "system" message with your knowledge base context. Without an OpenAI key, avatars only echo user input.

## Additional Features

The SDK includes callbacks for when avatars start/stop speaking and supports mid-sentence interruption.

content.content:
# HeyGen Streaming Avatar SDK Overview

## Purpose
The Streaming Avatar SDK enables developers to integrate HeyGen's Interactive Avatar into applications. It provides a TypeScript/JavaScript interface for creating and managing avatar sessions with voice and text interactions.

## Key Features

**Core Capabilities:**
- Real-time avatar streaming with configurable quality levels
- Voice chat support with multiple STT providers (Deepgram, Gladia)
- Text-based interaction modes
- Push-to-talk functionality (v2.1.0+)
- Session management with customizable idle timeouts

**Audio Configuration:**
- Multiple voice providers including ElevenLabs
- Rate control (0.5-1.5x) and emotion settings
- Input audio muting capabilities
- Silence prompt options

## Installation & Setup

```bash
npm install @heygen/streaming-avatar
npm run build
```

**Access Requirements:**
Users must obtain an API key or trial token from HeyGen's settings portal, then generate a streaming access token via the endpoint: `POST https://api.heygen.com/v1/streaming.create_token`

## Usage Pattern

Initialize the SDK, configure avatar parameters (quality, voice, STT settings), create a session, then manage interaction through speak commands or voice chat modes. Sessions automatically close after inactivity or manual termination.

## Current Status
The project is transitioning users toward LiveAvatar, with Interactive Avatar "deprecating in the near future."

Based on the search results, here's what I found about the terms in your query related to streaming avatars and media rendering:

## Key Concepts

**mediaStream**: A MediaStream is one of the objects that can serve as the source of media associated with an HTMLMediaElement, along with MediaSource, Blob, or File.

**srcObject**: The srcObject property of the HTMLMediaElement interface sets or returns the object which serves as the source of the media associated with the HTMLMediaElement. You can now set srcObject to the MediaStream directly, rather than using the older `URL.createObjectURL()` method.

**Practical Application**: The search results include an example of adding 3D avatars to live video streams. To integrate avatars, you create a video element and set its srcObject to a new MediaStream using camera track. Additionally, you can capture a canvas stream and create a custom video track from it to publish to a channel.

**Rendering**: To render a ThreeJS scene, you use a render loop and pass a function to update the head rotation and blend shape intensity using prediction results.

The search results don't show specific `getVideoElement` method implementations, but the overall pattern involves setting `srcObject` on video elements to render media streams and capturing canvas elements for rendering 3D avatar content.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have comprehensive information. Let me compile the full research report for you.

---

## HeyGen Streaming Avatar SDK Research Report

Based on my research, I need to clarify an important point: **Your project is using `@heygen/streaming-avatar` (v2.1.0), NOT the newer `@heygen/liveavatar-web-sdk`**. These are different products. The Streaming Avatar SDK is the current production version, with LiveAvatar being a newer upgrade path.

### 1. Full API Surface

#### **Initialization**

```typescript
import { StreamingAvatar, AvatarQuality, StreamingEvents, TaskType } from '@heygen/streaming-avatar';

const avatar = new StreamingAvatar({ token: 'access-token' });
```

The SDK requires a session token (not your API key) obtained from the create_token endpoint.

#### **Session Management**

- **`createStartAvatar(config)`**: Creates and starts a new streaming session
  - Parameters: `avatarName` (avatar ID), `quality` (High/Medium/Low), voice settings, knowledge base, STT config, language, idle timeout
  - Returns: `StartAvatarResponse` with session ID, access token, WebSocket URL, duration limits

- **`stopAvatar()`**: Terminates the active session cleanly

- **`newSession()`**: Alternative method to create and start a session

#### **Speaking/TTS**

- **`speak(config)`**: Sends text for avatar vocalization
  - Parameters: `{ text: string, taskType: TaskType.REPEAT | TaskType.TALK }`
  - `TaskType.REPEAT`: Avatar simply vocalizes the text
  - `TaskType.TALK`: Avatar generates responses via GPT-4o mini integration (requires knowledge base)
  - **Yes, TaskType.REPEAT is fully supported** (your current code uses this)

#### **Voice Chat**

- **`startVoiceChat()`**: Enables bidirectional voice interaction
- **`closeVoiceChat()`**: Stops voice chat mode
- **`startListening()` / `stopListening()`**: Controls audio input stream

#### **Interruption**

- **`interrupt()`**: Halts current speaking and clears queued speech

#### **Cleanup**

- Manual cleanup via `stopAvatar()` on unmount
- Sessions auto-close after ~10 minutes of inactivity (Trial Token limit)

---

### 2. Token Flow

#### **Token Creation (Backend)**

**Endpoint**: `POST https://api.heygen.com/v1/streaming.create_token`

**Headers**: `{ "x-api-key": HEYGEN_API_KEY }`

**Response**: `{ "data": { "token": "hg_..." } }`

Your backend (`/api/heygen/token`) implements this correctly:
```python
async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client:
    resp = await client.post(
        "https://api.heygen.com/v1/streaming.create_token",
        headers={"x-api-key": api_key},
    )
    return {"token": resp.json()["data"]["token"]}
```

#### **Session Start**

Frontend calls:
```typescript
const avatar = new StreamingAvatar({ token });
const sessionData = await avatar.createStartAvatar({
  avatarName: 'avatar-id',
  quality: AvatarQuality.Medium,
});
// sessionData contains: session_id, access_token, websocket_url, etc.
```

The SDK handles WebSocket connection via LiveKit internally. You don't call LiveKit directly.

#### **What LiveKit Returns**

The SDK abstracts LiveKit away. You get:
- Session metadata (ID, token, connection details)
- MediaStream object via the `STREAM_READY` event (see below)

---

### 3. Events/Callbacks Available

The SDK uses an event-driven pattern via the `on()` method. Your current code correctly implements these:

#### **Core Events**

| Event | When Fired | Handler Signature |
|-------|-----------|-------------------|
| `STREAM_READY` | MediaStream is ready for display | `(event: { detail: MediaStream }) => void` |
| `AVATAR_START_TALKING` | Avatar begins speaking | `() => void` |
| `AVATAR_STOP_TALKING` | Avatar finishes speaking | `() => void` |
| `STREAM_DISCONNECTED` | Session ends or connection drops | `() => void` |

#### **Additional Events (less common)**

- `AVATAR_TALKING_MESSAGE`: When avatar sends a speaking message
- `AVATAR_END_MESSAGE`: When avatar finishes sending messages
- `USER_START`: User begins input
- `USER_STOP`: User stops input
- `USER_SILENCE`: Silence detected during recording

**Event Registration**:
```typescript
avatar.on(StreamingEvents.STREAM_READY, (event) => { ... });
avatar.off(StreamingEvents.STREAM_READY, handler); // Remove listener
```

Your implementation correctly uses `STREAM_READY`, `AVATAR_START_TALKING`, `AVATAR_STOP_TALKING`, and `STREAM_DISCONNECTED`.

---

### 4. The `speak()` Method Signature

```typescript
await avatar.speak({
  text: string,
  taskType: TaskType.REPEAT | TaskType.TALK,
  // Optional:
  taskMode?: TaskMode.SYNC | TaskMode.ASYNC,
});
```

**TaskType.REPEAT Support**: ✅ **Yes, fully supported** (your code uses this)

**Your Implementation** (correct):
```typescript
await avatarRef.current.speak({ 
  text: next, 
  taskType: TaskType.REPEAT 
});
```

This matches the expected signature exactly.

---

### 5. Video/Media Stream Rendering

The `STREAM_READY` event provides a `MediaStream` object that contains the avatar video.

#### **Rendering Pattern**

```typescript
avatar.on(StreamingEvents.STREAM_READY, (event: { detail: MediaStream }) => {
  if (videoRef.current && event.detail) {
    videoRef.current.srcObject = event.detail;  // Assign MediaStream
    videoRef.current.play().catch(() => {});    // Start playback
  }
});
```

**Your Implementation** (in `useHeyGenAvatar.ts`, lines 84-90):
```typescript
avatar.on(StreamingEvents.STREAM_READY, (event: { detail: MediaStream }) => {
  if (videoRef.current && event.detail) {
    videoRef.current.srcObject = event.detail;
    videoRef.current.play().catch(() => {});
  }
  setIsReady(true);
});
```

This is **correct**. The `MediaStream` is then rendered in an HTML video element using the standard `srcObject` property.

---

### 6. Additional Configuration Options

#### **Avatar Quality**
- `AvatarQuality.High`: 720p / 2000 kbps
- `AvatarQuality.Medium`: 480p / 1000 kbps
- `AvatarQuality.Low`: 360p / 500 kbps

#### **Voice Settings**
- **Voice Provider**: Multiple including ElevenLabs
- **Voice Emotion**: EXCITED, SERIOUS, FRIENDLY, SOOTHING, BROADCASTER (voice-dependent)
- **Rate**: 0.5–1.5x speech rate control
- **Input Audio Muting**: Supported

#### **STT (Speech-to-Text) Configuration**
- **Providers**: Deepgram, Gladia (configurable in `createStartAvatar`)
- **Language**: Configurable

#### **Knowledge Base Integration**
- Required for `TaskType.TALK` mode
- Optional `KnowledgeID` or `KnowledgeBase` parameter to customize GPT-4o mini responses

---

### 7. Important Notes for Your Project

1. **Trial Token Limitations**:
   - Max 3 concurrent sessions
   - Auto-closes after 10 minutes of inactivity
   - No per-session charges

2. **Avatar Compatibility**:
   - Only **Finetune Instant** and **Studio Avatars** support streaming
   - Photo Avatars are incompatible

3. **Deprecation Path**:
   - HeyGen is transitioning to **LiveAvatar** (newer product)
   - Current Streaming Avatar SDK will "deprecate in the near future"
   - Your token flow and event patterns will likely differ in LiveAvatar

4. **Your Queue Implementation**:
   - Correctly processes one sentence at a time using `AVATAR_STOP_TALKING` event
   - Prevents "garbling" by waiting for previous speak() to complete
   - Proper error handling and cleanup

---

### Sources

- [Streaming Avatar SDK Reference | HeyGen Documentation](https://docs.heygen.com/docs/streaming-avatar-sdk-reference)
- [Streaming Avatar SDK | Real-Time AI Avatars | HeyGen](https://docs.heygen.com/docs/streaming-avatar-sdk)
- [GitHub - HeyGen-Official/StreamingAvatarSDK](https://github.com/HeyGen-Official/StreamingAvatarSDK)
- [GitHub - HeyGen-Official/StreamingAvatarTSDemo](https://github.com/HeyGen-Official/StreamingAvatarTSDemo)
- [@heygen/streaming-avatar npm package](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@heygen/streaming-avatar)
- [HeyGen Quick Start Guide](https://docs.heygen.com/docs/quick-start)
- [Create Session Token | HeyGen API Reference](https://docs.heygen.com/reference/create-session-token)
- [HTMLMediaElement: srcObject property - MDN Web Docs](https://developer.mozilla.REDACTED)

# FULL Mode Configuration Summary

Based on the documentation provided, here's what's available about LiveAvatar's FULL Mode:

## Configuration Layers

The page describes four customizable layers:

1. **Avatar (Visual)** - "Defines what the avatar looks like. Choose from a wide selection of avatars, each with unique styles, appearances, and expressions."

2. **Voice (Audio)** - "Defines what the avatar sounds like. Choose a voices that fit your needs — from calm and professional to energetic or youthful."

3. **Context (Cognitive)** - "Defines how the avatar thinks and responds. Control the personality traits, background knowledge, and behavior, which guide how the LLM generates responses."

4. **Interactivity Type (Conversational)** - Supports two modes:
   - **Conversational** (default): System manages when avatar responds based on speech pauses
   - **Push-to-Talk**: Developer has explicit control over when input is registered

## Available Settings

**Video Configuration:**
- Quality options: very_high (1080p), high (720p), medium (480p), low (360p)
- Encoding: VP8 or H264

## Important Limitation

The documentation **does not provide** code examples, API endpoints, or detailed instructions for:
- Creating sessions
- Sending text for avatar speech
- Accessing event details
- Retrieving video streams
- Stopping sessions

The page references related docs on "FULL Mode Lifecycle" and "FULL Mode Events" that would contain these implementation details, but they aren't included in the provided content.

content.content:
# FULL Mode Lifecycle Overview

Based on the documentation provided, here's what's covered:

## Session Lifecycle - Three Main Phases

**1. Starting a Session**
- Generate a session token configured for FULL mode
- Pass the token to your frontend application
- Start the session (either immediately via backend or after user interaction)
- Connect to the LiveAvatar-managed WebRTC room

**2. Managing the Session**
The documentation states: *"After connecting to the room, managing the session will be done purely via an event driven architecture."* Control includes:
- Speech timing (when user/avatar speaks)
- Transcript data
- Avatar behaviors (interrupting, idle/listening states)
- Direct avatar response control

**3. Ending the Session**
- Close the LiveKit room
- Avatar and user are removed
- Clean up external resources

## Architecture Recommendation

The docs recommend: *"making your frontend the primary controller for emitting events from the LiveAvatar room"* to minimize latency. Backend handles logging and analytics.

## Important Limitation

**The provided documentation does NOT include:**
- Actual code examples or method signatures
- API endpoint specifications
- Token generation implementation details
- Speech text submission methods
- Video stream handling code
- WebRTC connection specifics

The page references "FULL mode's event list" for detailed operations but those details aren't included in the source material provided. You'll need to consult the FULL Mode Events page for specific implementation code.

content.content:
# FULL Mode Events in LiveAvatar

## Command Events
Published to topic: `agent-control`

| Event | Payload | Purpose |
|-------|---------|---------|
| `avatar.interrupt` | N/A | "Interrupts and stops any scheduled commands" |
| `avatar.speak_text` | `{"text": string}` | "Instructs the avatar to say the spoken text" |
| `avatar.speak_response` | `{"text": string}` | "Instruct the avatar to generate an LLM response" |
| `avatar.start_listening` | N/A | Switches avatar to listening mode |
| `avatar.stop_listening` | N/A | "Returns to the idle state" |
| `user.start_push_to_talk` | N/A | Push-To-Talk only: start audio capture |
| `user.stop_push_to_talk` | N/A | Push-To-Talk only: stop audio capture |

## Server Events
Emitted to topic: `agent-response`

| Event | Payload | Trigger |
|-------|---------|---------|
| `user.speak_started` | N/A | User sends audio data |
| `user.speak_ended` | N/A | "Always follows a `user.speak_started` event" |
| `avatar.speak_started` | N/A | Avatar begins audio output |
| `avatar.speak_ended` | N/A | Avatar finishes speaking |
| `user.transcription` | `{"text": string}` | Audio processing complete |
| `avatar.transcription` | `{"text": string}` | Avatar text response generated |
| `user.push_to_talk_started` | N/A | PTT start processed successfully |
| `user.push_to_talk_start_failed` | N/A | PTT start failed |
| `user.push_to_talk_stop_failed` | N/A | PTT stop failed |
| `session.stopped` | `{"end_reason": string}` | Session ends |

## Event Structure
All events follow JSON format:
```json
{
  "event_type": "string",
  "session_id": "string"
}
```

**Note:** The documentation provided does not include code examples for event listeners. You'll need to reference LiveKit protocol documentation for implementation details.

content.content:
# HeyGen to LiveAvatar Migration Guide Summary

Based on the documentation provided, here's what the migration guide covers:

## Migration Overview

The guide outlines three main steps:

1. **Copy over assets** from HeyGen to LiveAvatar
2. **Transition your implementation** (subscription + API updates)
3. **Clean up HeyGen subscription**

## Asset Migration

The platform automatically detects HeyGen assets (avatars, voices, knowledge bases) and enables migration. Key points:

- Assets are **copied** to LiveAvatar—changes in one platform don't sync to the other
- "We do not support migrating individual voices at the current point in time"
- Knowledge bases are renamed to "contexts" in LiveAvatar

## Subscription & Timeline

After asset migration, users receive a **30-day temporary LiveAvatar subscription** to test the new implementation before committing.

## Code Examples & SDK Mapping

**The documentation does NOT include specific SDK method mappings or code examples.** Instead, it directs users to a separate comparison page: "LiveAvatar vs HeyGen Interactive Avatar" for detailed API changes.

## Key Recommendation

"When comparing on a per minute basis, LiveAvatar's is tends to be much cheaper than HeyGen. Hence, we strongly recommend converting as soon as possible."

## Limitation

The provided content links to detailed API comparisons but doesn't display the actual method mappings or code samples. You would need to visit the referenced comparison page for specific SDK changes.

input.prompt: Show me the complete comparison between LiveAvatar and HeyGen Interactive Avatar. What are the specific API/SDK differences? Method mappings? Code examples? How does the new SDK compare to @heygen/streaming-avatar?
Show me everything about sandbox/development mode: how to test without credits, how to set up, any limitations.

content.content:
# Sandbox Mode for LiveAvatar Development

## Overview
Sandbox Mode is a specialized execution environment designed to help developers integrate and test LiveAvatar without consuming credits. It mirrors production behavior while applying strict resource limitations.

## Key Features

**Cost-Free Testing**
"Sandbox mode does not require any credits to start and will not consume any credits." This allows you to iterate freely on your integration.

**Primary Use Cases**
- Validating API integrations
- Testing session lifecycle workflows
- Iterating on frontend/backend code
- Debugging audio/video pipelines

## Important Limitations

**Avatar Restrictions**
Only one avatar is available in sandbox: Wayne (ID: `dd73ea75-1218-4ef3-92ce-606d5f7fbc0a`). You cannot test with your full production avatar suite.

**Session Duration Caps**
"Sandbox mode sessions will terminate in around 1 minute." Sessions automatically close when reaching maximum duration, so you're limited for extended testing scenarios.

## Setup Instructions

To enable Sandbox Mode, include the `is_sandbox` flag when creating a LiveAvatar session token through the Create Session Token endpoint.

## Bottom Line

Sandbox Mode is ideal for development validation and debugging without financial risk, but the one-minute session limit and single avatar option mean you'll eventually need production testing before full deployment.

content.content:
# LiveAvatar vs HeyGen Interactive Avatar: Complete Comparison

## Starting a Session

**HeyGen (3 API calls):**
- Create Session Token
- New Session
- Start Session

**LiveAvatar (2 API calls):**
- Create Session Token (unified logic)
- Start Session

The documentation notes: *"We realized that having to call three APIs in a specific order to configure and initiate the session was a tad confusing."* LiveAvatar simplified this workflow by consolidating operations.

**Migration recommendation:** Call Create Session Token on your backend, pass session data to the client, then start the session from your frontend using the client token.

## Session Management

| HeyGen | LiveAvatar |
|--------|-----------|
| Close Session | Stop Session |
| Keep Alive | Keep Session Alive |
| List Sessions (Active) | List Sessions (with `type` parameter) |
| List Sessions (History) | List Sessions (with `type` parameter) |

**Deprecated endpoints:** Send Task and Interrupt Task are no longer available.

**New approach:** LiveAvatar uses *"a fully event-driven architecture for controlling sessions"* instead of task-based REST endpoints, improving responsiveness and reducing latency.

## Task Command Mapping

- `repeat` → `avatar.speak_text`
- `chat` → `avatar.speak_response`
- `interrupt` → `avatar.interrupt`

## Avatar Endpoints

LiveAvatar split the avatar list into:
- **List Public Avatars**
- **List Private Avatars**

Call both endpoints to fetch all accessible avatars.

## Knowledge Base → Context Rename

| HeyGen | LiveAvatar |
|--------|-----------|
| Create a Knowledge Base | Create Context |
| List Knowledge Bases | List User Contexts |
| Update Knowledge Base | Update Context |
| Delete Knowledge Base | Delete Context |

## Key Architectural Differences

The primary shift is from *"task-based REST endpoints"* to event-driven control, eliminating separate interrupt/task operations in favor of unified command events. This represents a fundamental redesign philosophy rather than simple endpoint renaming.

# LiveAvatar Web SDK

## About

This is offical LiveAvatar supported client-side SDK.
This SDK library manages various LiveAvatar sessions, supporting the session initiation,
session management, and session cleanup.

## API Documentation

Please refer to us here https://docs.liveavatar.com

## Installation

Install the package in your project through package manager.

```bash
npm install @heygen/liveavatar-web-sdk
# or
pnpm install @heygen/liveavatar-web-sdk
```

## Usage

This library is meant for development use in various client-side facing JavaScript projects.
It's tailored to manage LiveAvatar sessions, handling the complexities of starting, stopping and various avatar actions. With just the session token, we help focus on the session complexity so you focus on building something great.

## Example Usage

```ts
import { LiveAvatarSession } from "@heygen/liveavatar-web-sdk";

// Make a backend call to grab the sessionToken
const { sessionToken } = await myBackendCallForSessionToken();
const userConfig = {
  voiceChat: true,
};

const session = new LiveAvatarSession(sessionToken, userConfig);

// Start the session
await session.start();

// Build something great with LiveAvatar

// Close the session
await session.stop();
```

## License

LiveAvatar Web SDK is licensed under the MIT License.

Please refer to the LICENSE file for more information.

# LiveAvatar Web SDK

## About

This is offical LiveAvatar supported client-side SDK.
This SDK library manages various LiveAvatar sessions, supporting the session initiation,
session management, and session cleanup.

## API Documentation

Please refer to us here https://docs.liveavatar.com

## Installation

Install the package in your project through package manager.

```bash
npm install @heygen/liveavatar-web-sdk
# or
pnpm install @heygen/liveavatar-web-sdk
```

## Usage

This library is meant for development use in various client-side facing JavaScript projects.
It's tailored to manage LiveAvatar sessions, handling the complexities of starting, stopping and various avatar actions. With just the session token, we help focus on the session complexity so you focus on building something great.

## Example Usage

```ts
import { LiveAvatarSession } from "@heygen/liveavatar-web-sdk";

// Make a backend call to grab the sessionToken
const { sessionToken } = await myBackendCallForSessionToken();
const userConfig = {
  voiceChat: true,
};

const session = new LiveAvatarSession(sessionToken, userConfig);

// Start the session
await session.start();

// Build something great with LiveAvatar

// Close the session
await session.stop();
```

## License

LiveAvatar Web SDK is licensed under the MIT License.

Please refer to the LICENSE file for more information.

content.content:
# LiveAvatar API Reference Endpoints

## Sessions
- **Create Session Token** - `POST /v1/sessions/token`
- **Start Session** - `POST /v1/sessions/start`
- **Stop Session** - `POST /v1/sessions/stop`
- **Keep Session Alive** - `POST /v1/sessions/keep-alive`
- **List Sessions** - `GET /v1/sessions`
- **Get Session Transcript** - `GET /v1/sessions/{session_id}/transcript`

## Avatar
- **List Public Avatars** - `GET /v1/avatars/public`
- **List User Avatars** - `GET /v1/avatars`
- **Get Avatar By Id** - `GET /v1/avatars/{avatar_id}`
- **Update Avatar** - `PATCH /v1/avatars/{avatar_id}`
- **Delete Avatar** - `DELETE /v1/avatars/{avatar_id}`

## Contexts
- **Create Context** - `POST /v1/contexts`
- **List User Contexts** - `GET /v1/contexts`
- **Get Context By Id** - `GET /v1/contexts/{context_id}`
- **Update Context** - `PATCH /v1/contexts/{context_id}`
- **Delete Context** - `DELETE /v1/contexts/{context_id}`

## Voices
- **List Voices** - `GET /v1/voices`
- **Get Voice By Id** - `GET /v1/voices/{voice_id}`

The endpoints you're looking for are the first two under Sessions.

content.content:
# LiveAvatar Quickstart: Complete Flow

## 1. Create Session Token

**Endpoint:** `POST https://api.liveavatar.com/v1/sessions/token`

**Request:**
```bash
curl --request POST \
     --url https://api.liveavatar.com/v1/sessions/token \
     --header 'X-API-KEY: <API Token>' \
     --header 'accept: application/json' \
     --header 'content-type: application/json' \
     --data '{
        "mode": "FULL",
        "avatar_id": <avatar_id>,
        "avatar_persona": {
          "voice_id": <voice_id>,
          "context_id": <context_id>,
          "language": "en"
         }
      }'
```

**Response:**
```json
{
  "session_id": <new_session_id>,
  "session_token": <your_session_token>
}
```

## 2. Start the Session

**Endpoint:** `POST https://api.liveavatar.com/v1/sessions/start`

**Request:**
```bash
curl --request POST \
     --url https://api.liveavatar.com/v1/sessions/start \
     --header 'accept: application/json' \
     --header 'authorization: Bearer <session_token>'
```

**Response returns:** LiveKit room URL and room token.

## 3. Join LiveKit Room

Navigate to:
```
https://meet.livekit.io/custom?liveKitUrl=[livekit_url]&token=[livekit_client_token]
```

You can now interact with the avatar in the browser.

## Key Parameters

- `mode`: "FULL" (or "LITE")
- `avatar_id`: Your avatar identifier
- `voice_id`: Voice configuration
- `context_id`: Context configuration
- `language`: "en"

input.prompt:
Design an implementation plan for migrating from HeyGen Streaming Avatar SDK to HeyGen LiveAvatar SDK, including full mirror page re-integration.

## Current State

The mirror page (`frontend/src/app/mirror/page.tsx`) has been stripped down to just camera + gesture recognition. It has NO avatar, session lifecycle, voice, product carousel, or SentenceBuffer integration. Here's what it looks like now (148 lines):
- Camera feed (mirrored) with webcam
- Gesture recognition (MediaPipe)
- Snapshot handler (captures camera frame on `request_snapshot` socket event)
- No avatar, no session state, no voice, no product carousel

The old hook `frontend/src/hooks/useHeyGenAvatar.ts` still exists but uses the deprecated `@heygen/streaming-avatar` SDK.

The backend endpoint `backend/routers/heygen.py` calls the old `https://api.heygen.com/v1/streaming.create_token` endpoint.

## LiveAvatar SDK API (from reading type definitions of @heygen/liveavatar-web-sdk@0.0.10)

### Key class: `LiveAvatarSession`
```typescript
constructor(sessionAccessToken: string, config?: SessionConfig)
// SessionConfig: { voiceChat?: VoiceChatConfig | boolean; apiUrl?: string }

// Lifecycle
start(): Promise<void>           // Starts session (calls /sessions/start internally)
stop(): Promise<void>            // Ends session
keepAlive(): Promise<void>       // Extends session

// Media
attach(element: HTMLMediaElement): void  // Attaches video/audio to DOM element

// Speaking
repeat(message: string): void    // Say text verbatim (replaces TaskType.REPEAT)
message(message: string): void   // Get LLM response (replaces TaskType.TALK)
repeatAudio(audio: string): void // Play audio

// Control
interrupt(): void
startListening(): void
stopListening(): void

// State
get state(): SessionState  // INACTIVE | CONNECTING | CONNECTED | DISCONNECTING | DISCONNECTED
get voiceChat(): VoiceChat
get connectionQuality(): ConnectionQuality
get maxSessionDuration(): number | null
```

### Session events (SessionEvent enum):
- `session.state_changed` → (state: SessionState) => void
- `session.stream_ready` → () => void
- `session.connection_quality_changed` → (quality: ConnectionQuality) => void
- `session.disconnected` → (reason: SessionDisconnectReason) => void

### Agent events (AgentEventsEnum):
- `avatar.speak_started` → (event) => void
- `avatar.speak_ended` → (event) => void
- `user.speak_started` → (event) => void
- `user.speak_ended` → (event) => void
- `user.transcription` → (event: { text: string }) => void
- `avatar.transcription` → (event: { text: string }) => void

### Token flow (backend):
```
POST https://api.liveavatar.com/v1/sessions/token
Header: X-API-KEY: <api-key>
Body: { "mode": "FULL", "avatar_id": <id>, "avatar_persona": { "voice_id": <id>, "context_id": <id>, "language": "en" } }
Response: { "session_id": <id>, "session_token": <token> }
```

The SDK's `SessionAPIClient` handles `/sessions/start` internally when you call `session.start()`.

### Key difference: `attach(element)` replaces manual MediaStream handling
Instead of listening for STREAM_READY and manually setting `videoRef.srcObject = event.detail`, you call `session.attach(videoElement)` and the SDK handles it.

## Existing Components that Need Re-integration

- `frontend/src/components/mirror/AvatarPiP.tsx` — PiP video overlay (may need small update for attach pattern)
- `frontend/src/components/mirror/VoiceIndicator.tsx` — Shows listening state + interim transcript
- `frontend/src/components/mirror/ProductCarousel.tsx` — Product cards from Poke
- `frontend/src/lib/sentence-buffer.ts` — Chunks mira_speech into sentences for avatar.speak()

## Backend Token Endpoint

Current: `GET /api/heygen/token` → calls old HeyGen API
New: `POST /api/heygen/token` → calls `https://api.liveavatar.com/v1/sessions/token`

- `LIVEAVATAR_AVATAR_ID` or `NEXT_PUBLIC_LIVEAVATAR_AVATAR_ID`
- `LIVEAVATAR_VOICE_ID`
- `LIVEAVATAR_CONTEXT_ID`

`backend/tests/test_heygen_routes.py` needs updating for new endpoint URL/format.

## Sandbox mode
LiveAvatar supports sandbox mode (free, 1-min sessions) by passing `"is_sandbox": true` in the token request. This is useful for development.

## Project Conventions
- Inline styles (no CSS framework)

stop(): Promise<void>            // Ends session

repeatAudio(audio: string): void // Play audio

stopListening(): void

// State

get state(): SessionState  // INACTIVE | CONNECTING | CONNECTED | DISCONNECTING | DISCONNECTED
get voiceChat(): VoiceChat
get connectionQuality(): ConnectionQuality
get maxSessionDuration(): number | null
```

### Session events (SessionEvent enum):
- `session.state_changed` → (state: SessionState) => void
- `session.stream_ready` → () => void
- `session.connection_quality_changed` → (quality: ConnectionQuality) => void
- `session.disconnected` → (reason: SessionDisconnectReason) => void

### Agent events (AgentEventsEnum):
- `avatar.speak_started` → (event) => void
- `avatar.speak_ended` → (event) => void
- `user.speak_started` → (event) => void
- `user.speak_ended` → (event) => void
- `user.transcription` → (event: { text: string }) => void
- `avatar.transcription` → (event: { text: string }) => void

### Token flow (backend):
```
POST https://api.liveavatar.com/v1/sessions/token
Header: X-API-KEY: <api-key>
Body: { "mode": "FULL", "avatar_id": <id>, "avatar_persona": { "voice_id": <id>, "context_id": <id>, "language": "en" } }
Response: { "session_id": <id>, "session_token": <token> }
```

The SDK's `SessionAPIClient` handles `/sessions/start` internally when you call `session.start()`.

### Key difference: `attach(element)` replaces manual MediaStream handling
Instead of listening for STREAM_READY and manually setting `videoRef.srcObject = event.detail`, you call `session.attach(videoElement)` and the SDK handles it.

## Existing Components that Need Re-integration

These components exist and should be brought back into the mirror page:
- `frontend/src/components/mirror/AvatarPiP.tsx` — PiP video overlay (may need small update for attach pattern)
- `frontend/src/components/mirror/VoiceIndicator.tsx` — Shows listening state + interim transcript
- `frontend/src/components/mirror/ProductCarousel.tsx` — Product cards from Poke
- `frontend/src/lib/sentence-buffer.ts` — Chunks mira_speech into sentences for avatar.speak()
- `frontend/src/hooks/useDeepgramSTT.ts` — Deepgram streaming STT (MAY be replaceable by LiveAvatar's built-in voice chat)

The SentenceBuffer feeds sentences to `avatar.speak()`. With LiveAvatar, this becomes `session.repeat(sentence)`.

## Backend Token Endpoint

Current: `GET /api/heygen/token` → calls old HeyGen API
New: `POST /api/heygen/token` → calls `https://api.liveavatar.com/v1/sessions/token`

The endpoint needs to accept avatar config (avatar_id, voice_id, context_id) either from env vars or request body, and return `{ session_token, session_id }`.

## Environment Variables
Old: `HEYGEN_API_KEY` (used with old HeyGen API)
New: Same env var name, but the key is for LiveAvatar. User also needs:
- `LIVEAVATAR_AVATAR_ID` or `NEXT_PUBLIC_LIVEAVATAR_AVATAR_ID`
- `LIVEAVATAR_VOICE_ID`
- `LIVEAVATAR_CONTEXT_ID`

## Backend tests
`backend/tests/test_heygen_routes.py` needs updating for new endpoint URL/format.

## Sandbox mode
LiveAvatar supports sandbox mode (free, 1-min sessions) by passing `"is_sandbox": true` in the token request. This is useful for development.

## Project Conventions
- Inline styles (no CSS framework)
- Frontend: TypeScript strict mode
- Backend: Python 3.11+ / FastAPI
- Socket.io events: snake_case
- Existing tests: Vitest (frontend), pytest (backend)

## Plan Requirements
1. Rewrite `useHeyGenAvatar.ts` → `useLiveAvatar.ts` using new SDK
2. Update backend token endpoint for LiveAvatar API
3. Re-integrate avatar + session lifecycle + voice + products into mirror page
4. Add Start Session button (from earlier plan)
5. Update package.json (remove old SDK, add new)
6. Update backend tests
7. Keep the same public hook interface where possible so mirror page integration is clean

content.content:
1→"""HeyGen streaming avatar token endpoint."""
     2→
     3→import os
     4→
     5→import httpx
     6→from fastapi import APIRouter, HTTPException
     7→
     8→router = APIRouter(prefix="/api/heygen", tags=["heygen"])
     9→
    10→
    11→@router.get("/token")
    12→async def get_heygen_token():
    13→    """Generate a short-lived HeyGen session token for the streaming avatar SDK."""
    14→    api_key = os.environ.get("HEYGEN_API_KEY")
    15→    if not api_key:
    16→        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail="HEYGEN_API_KEY not configured")
    17→
    18→    async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client:
    19→        resp = await client.post(
    20→            "https://api.heygen.com/v1/streaming.create_token",
    21→            headers={"x-api-key": api_key},
    22→        )
    23→
    24→    if resp.status_code != 200:
    25→        raise HTTPException(status_code=502, detail=f"HeyGen API error: {resp.text}")
    26→
    27→    return {"token": resp.json()["data"]["token"]}
    28→

Design an implementation plan for migrating from HeyGen Streaming Avatar SDK to HeyGen LiveAvatar SDK, including full mirror page re-integration.

## Current State

The mirror page (`frontend/src/app/mirror/page.tsx`) has been stripped down to just camera + gesture recognition. It has NO avatar, session lifecycle, voice, product carousel, or SentenceBuffer integration. Here's what it looks like now (148 lines):
- Camera feed (mirrored) with webcam
- Gesture recognition (MediaPipe)
- Snapshot handler (captures camera frame on `request_snapshot` socket event)
- No avatar, no session state, no voice, no product carousel

The old hook `frontend/src/hooks/useHeyGenAvatar.ts` still exists but uses the deprecated `@heygen/streaming-avatar` SDK.

The backend endpoint `backend/routers/heygen.py` calls the old `https://api.heygen.com/v1/streaming.create_token` endpoint.

## LiveAvatar SDK API (from reading type definitions of @heygen/liveavatar-web-sdk@0.0.10)

### Key class: `LiveAvatarSession`
```typescript
constructor(sessionAccessToken: string, config?: SessionConfig)
// SessionConfig: { voiceChat?: VoiceChatConfig | boolean; apiUrl?: string }

// Lifecycle
start(): Promise<void>           // Starts session (calls /sessions/start internally)
stop(): Promise<void>            // Ends session
keepAlive(): Promise<void>       // Extends session

// Media
attach(element: HTMLMediaElement): void  // Attaches video/audio to DOM element

// Speaking
repeat(message: string): void    // Say text verbatim (replaces TaskType.REPEAT)
message(message: string): void   // Get LLM response (replaces TaskType.TALK)
repeatAudio(audio: string): void // Play audio

// Control
interrupt(): void
startListening(): void
stopListening(): void

// State
get state(): SessionState  // INACTIVE | CONNECTING | CONNECTED | DISCONNECTING | DISCONNECTED
get voiceChat(): VoiceChat
get connectionQuality(): ConnectionQuality
get maxSessionDuration(): number | null
```

### Session events (SessionEvent enum):
- `session.state_changed` → (state: SessionState) => void
- `session.stream_ready` → () => void
- `session.connection_quality_changed` → (quality: ConnectionQuality) => void
- `session.disconnected` → (reason: SessionDisconnectReason) => void

### Agent events (AgentEventsEnum):
- `avatar.speak_started` → (event) => void
- `avatar.speak_ended` → (event) => void
- `user.speak_started` → (event) => void
- `user.speak_ended` → (event) => void
- `user.transcription` → (event: { text: string }) => void
- `avatar.transcription` → (event: { text: string }) => void

### Token flow (backend):
```
POST https://api.liveavatar.com/v1/sessions/token
Header: X-API-KEY: <api-key>
Body: { "mode": "FULL", "avatar_id": <id>, "avatar_persona": { "voice_id": <id>, "context_id": <id>, "language": "en" } }
Response: { "session_id": <id>, "session_token": <token> }
```

The SDK's `SessionAPIClient` handles `/sessions/start` internally when you call `session.start()`.

### Key difference: `attach(element)` replaces manual MediaStream handling
Instead of listening for STREAM_READY and manually setting `videoRef.srcObject = event.detail`, you call `session.attach(videoElement)` and the SDK handles it.

## Existing Components that Need Re-integration

These components exist and should be brought back into the mirror page:
- `frontend/src/components/mirror/AvatarPiP.tsx` — PiP video overlay (may need small update for attach pattern)
- `frontend/src/components/mirror/VoiceIndicator.tsx` — Shows listening state + interim transcript
- `frontend/src/components/mirror/ProductCarousel.tsx` — Product cards from Poke
- `frontend/src/lib/sentence-buffer.ts` — Chunks mira_speech into sentences for avatar.speak()
- `frontend/src/hooks/useDeepgramSTT.ts` — Deepgram streaming STT (MAY be replaceable by LiveAvatar's built-in voice chat)

The SentenceBuffer feeds sentences to `avatar.speak()`. With LiveAvatar, this becomes `session.repeat(sentence)`.

## Backend Token Endpoint

Current: `GET /api/heygen/token` → calls old HeyGen API
New: `POST /api/heygen/token` → calls `https://api.liveavatar.com/v1/sessions/token`

The endpoint needs to accept avatar config (avatar_id, voice_id, context_id) either from env vars or request body, and return `{ session_token, session_id }`.

## Environment Variables
Old: `HEYGEN_API_KEY` (used with old HeyGen API)
New: Same env var name, but the key is for LiveAvatar. User also needs:
- `LIVEAVATAR_AVATAR_ID` or `NEXT_PUBLIC_LIVEAVATAR_AVATAR_ID`
- `LIVEAVATAR_VOICE_ID`
- `LIVEAVATAR_CONTEXT_ID`

## Backend tests
`backend/tests/test_heygen_routes.py` needs updating for new endpoint URL/format.

## Sandbox mode
LiveAvatar supports sandbox mode (free, 1-min sessions) by passing `"is_sandbox": true` in the token request. This is useful for development.

- Inline styles (no CSS framework)

- `user.speak_ended` → (event) => void

- `avatar.transcription` → (event: { text: string }) => void

Header: X-API-KEY: <api-key>

## LiveAvatar SDK API (from reading type definitions of @heygen/liveavatar-web-sdk@0.0.10)

### Key class: `LiveAvatarSession`
```typescript
constructor(sessionAccessToken: string, config?: SessionConfig)
// SessionConfig: { voiceChat?: VoiceChatConfig | boolean; apiUrl?: string }

// Lifecycle
start(): Promise<void>           // Starts session (calls /sessions/start internally)
stop(): Promise<void>            // Ends session
keepAlive(): Promise<void>       // Extends session

// Media
attach(element: HTMLMediaElement): void  // Attaches video/audio to DOM element

// Speaking
repeat(message: string): void    // Say text verbatim (replaces TaskType.REPEAT)
message(message: string): void   // Get LLM response (replaces TaskType.TALK)
repeatAudio(audio: string): void // Play audio

// Control
interrupt(): void
startListening(): void
stopListening(): void

// State
get state(): SessionState  // INACTIVE | CONNECTING | CONNECTED | DISCONNECTING | DISCONNECTED
get voiceChat(): VoiceChat
get connectionQuality(): ConnectionQuality
get maxSessionDuration(): number | null
```

### Session events (SessionEvent enum):
- `session.state_changed` → (state: SessionState) => void
- `session.stream_ready` → () => void
- `session.connection_quality_changed` → (quality: ConnectionQuality) => void
- `session.disconnected` → (reason: SessionDisconnectReason) => void

### Agent events (AgentEventsEnum):
- `avatar.speak_started` → (event) => void
- `avatar.speak_ended` → (event) => void
- `user.speak_started` → (event) => void
- `user.speak_ended` → (event) => void
- `user.transcription` → (event: { text: string }) => void
- `avatar.transcription` → (event: { text: string }) => void

### Token flow (backend):
```
POST https://api.liveavatar.com/v1/sessions/token
Header: X-API-KEY: <api-key>
Body: { "mode": "FULL", "avatar_id": <id>, "avatar_persona": { "voice_id": <id>, "context_id": <id>, "language": "en" } }

```

### Key difference: `attach(element)` replaces manual MediaStream handling
Instead of listening for STREAM_READY and manually setting `videoRef.srcObject = event.detail`, you call `session.attach(videoElement)` and the SDK handles it.

- `frontend/src/components/mirror/AvatarPiP.tsx` — PiP video overlay (may need small update for attach pattern)

## Backend Token Endpoint

Current: `GET /api/heygen/token` → calls old HeyGen API

- `LIVEAVATAR_AVATAR_ID` or `NEXT_PUBLIC_LIVEAVATAR_AVATAR_ID`
- `LIVEAVATAR_VOICE_ID`
- `LIVEAVATAR_CONTEXT_ID`

## Backend tests
`backend/tests/test_heygen_routes.py` needs updating for new endpoint URL/format.

LiveAvatar supports sandbox mode (free, 1-min sessions) by passing `"is_sandbox": true` in the token request. This is useful for development.

- Inline styles (no CSS framework)
- Frontend: TypeScript strict mode
- Backend: Python 3.11+ / FastAPI
- Socket.io events: snake_case
- Existing tests: Vitest (frontend), pytest (backend)

1. Rewrite `useHeyGenAvatar.ts` → `useLiveAvatar.ts` using new SDK
2. Update backend token endpoint for LiveAvatar API
3. Re-integrate avatar + session lifecycle + voice + products into mirror page
4. Add Start Session button (from earlier plan)
5. Update package.json (remove old SDK, add new)
6. Update backend tests
7. Keep the same public hook interface where possible so mirror page integration is clean

Design a concrete, step-by-step implementation plan.

     2→
     3→import pytest
     4→from unittest.mock import patch, AsyncMock, MagicMock
     5→from httpx import AsyncClient, ASGITransport
     6→from main import app
     7→
     8→transport = ASGITransport(app=app)
     9→
    10→
    11→@pytest.mark.asyncio
    12→async def test_get_heygen_token_success():
    13→    """GET /api/heygen/token returns a session token from HeyGen API."""
    14→    mock_response = MagicMock()
    15→    mock_response.status_code = 200
    16→    mock_response.json.return_value = {"data": {"token": "hg_test_token_123"}}
    17→
    18→    with patch("routers.heygen.httpx.AsyncClient") as MockClient:
    19→        instance = AsyncMock()
    20→        instance.post.return_value = mock_response
    21→        instance.__aenter__ = AsyncMock(return_value=instance)
    22→        instance.__aexit__ = AsyncMock(return_value=False)
    23→        MockClient.return_value = instance
    24→
    25→        async with AsyncClient(transport=transport, base_url="http://test") as ac:
    26→            response = await ac.get("/api/heygen/token")
    27→
    28→    assert response.status_code == 200
    29→    assert response.json()["token"] == "hg_test_token_123"
    30→
    31→
    32→@pytest.mark.asyncio
    33→async def test_get_heygen_token_missing_api_key():
    34→    """Returns 500 when HEYGEN_API_KEY is not set."""
    35→    with patch("routers.heygen.os.environ.get", return_value=None):
    36→        async with AsyncClient(transport=transport, base_url="http://test") as ac:
    37→            response = await ac.get("/api/heygen/token")
    38→
    39→    assert response.status_code == 500
    40→
    41→
    42→@pytest.mark.asyncio

    44→    """Returns 502 when HeyGen API returns an error."""
    45→    mock_response = MagicMock()
    46→    mock_response.status_code = 401
    47→    mock_response.text = "Unauthorized"
    48→
    49→    with patch("routers.heygen.httpx.AsyncClient") as MockClient:
    50→        instance = AsyncMock()
    51→        instance.post.return_value = mock_response
    52→        instance.__aenter__ = AsyncMock(return_value=instance)
    53→        instance.__aexit__ = AsyncMock(return_value=False)

    55→
    56→        async with AsyncClient(transport=transport, base_url="http://test") as ac:
    57→            response = await ac.get("/api/heygen/token")

    59→    assert response.status_code == 502

Design a concrete, step-by-step implementation plan.

Design an implementation plan for migrating from HeyGen Streaming Avatar SDK to HeyGen LiveAvatar SDK, including full mirror page re-integration.

## Current State

The mirror page (`frontend/src/app/mirror/page.tsx`) has been stripped down to just camera + gesture recognition. It has NO avatar, session lifecycle, voice, product carousel, or SentenceBuffer integration. Here's what it looks like now (148 lines):
- Camera feed (mirrored) with webcam
- Gesture recognition (MediaPipe)
- Snapshot handler (captures camera frame on `request_snapshot` socket event)
- No avatar, no session state, no voice, no product carousel

The old hook `frontend/src/hooks/useHeyGenAvatar.ts` still exists but uses the deprecated `@heygen/streaming-avatar` SDK.

The backend endpoint `backend/routers/heygen.py` calls the old `https://api.heygen.com/v1/streaming.create_token` endpoint.

## LiveAvatar SDK API (from reading type definitions of @heygen/liveavatar-web-sdk@0.0.10)

### Key class: `LiveAvatarSession`
```typescript
constructor(sessionAccessToken: string, config?: SessionConfig)
// SessionConfig: { voiceChat?: VoiceChatConfig | boolean; apiUrl?: string }

// Lifecycle
start(): Promise<void>           // Starts session (calls /sessions/start internally)
stop(): Promise<void>            // Ends session
keepAlive(): Promise<void>       // Extends session

// Media
attach(element: HTMLMediaElement): void  // Attaches video/audio to DOM element

// Speaking
repeat(message: string): void    // Say text verbatim (replaces TaskType.REPEAT)
message(message: string): void   // Get LLM response (replaces TaskType.TALK)
repeatAudio(audio: string): void // Play audio

// Control
interrupt(): void
startListening(): void
stopListening(): void

// State
get state(): SessionState  // INACTIVE | CONNECTING | CONNECTED | DISCONNECTING | DISCONNECTED
get voiceChat(): VoiceChat
get connectionQuality(): ConnectionQuality
get maxSessionDuration(): number | null
```

### Session events (SessionEvent enum):
- `session.state_changed` → (state: SessionState) => void
- `session.stream_ready` → () => void
- `session.connection_quality_changed` → (quality: ConnectionQuality) => void
- `session.disconnected` → (reason: SessionDisconnectReason) => void

### Agent events (AgentEventsEnum):
- `avatar.speak_started` → (event) => void
- `avatar.speak_ended` → (event) => void
- `user.speak_started` → (event) => void
- `user.speak_ended` → (event) => void
- `user.transcription` → (event: { text: string }) => void
- `avatar.transcription` → (event: { text: string }) => void

### Token flow (backend):
```
POST https://api.liveavatar.com/v1/sessions/token
Header: X-API-KEY: <api-key>
Body: { "mode": "FULL", "avatar_id": <id>, "avatar_persona": { "voice_id": <id>, "context_id": <id>, "language": "en" } }
Response: { "session_id": <id>, "session_token": <token> }
```

The SDK's `SessionAPIClient` handles `/sessions/start` internally when you call `session.start()`.

### Key difference: `attach(element)` replaces manual MediaStream handling
Instead of listening for STREAM_READY and manually setting `videoRef.srcObject = event.detail`, you call `session.attach(videoElement)` and the SDK handles it.

## Existing Components that Need Re-integration

These components exist and should be brought back into the mirror page:
- `frontend/src/components/mirror/AvatarPiP.tsx` — PiP video overlay (may need small update for attach pattern)
- `frontend/src/components/mirror/VoiceIndicator.tsx` — Shows listening state + interim transcript
- `frontend/src/components/mirror/ProductCarousel.tsx` — Product cards from Poke
- `frontend/src/lib/sentence-buffer.ts` — Chunks mira_speech into sentences for avatar.speak()
- `frontend/src/hooks/useDeepgramSTT.ts` — Deepgram streaming STT (MAY be replaceable by LiveAvatar's built-in voice chat)

The SentenceBuffer feeds sentences to `avatar.speak()`. With LiveAvatar, this becomes `session.repeat(sentence)`.

## Backend Token Endpoint

Current: `GET /api/heygen/token` → calls old HeyGen API
New: `POST /api/heygen/token` → calls `https://api.liveavatar.com/v1/sessions/token`

The endpoint needs to accept avatar config (avatar_id, voice_id, context_id) either from env vars or request body, and return `{ session_token, session_id }`.

## Environment Variables
Old: `HEYGEN_API_KEY` (used with old HeyGen API)
New: Same env var name, but the key is for LiveAvatar. User also needs:
- `LIVEAVATAR_AVATAR_ID` or `NEXT_PUBLIC_LIVEAVATAR_AVATAR_ID`
- `LIVEAVATAR_VOICE_ID`
- `LIVEAVATAR_CONTEXT_ID`

## Backend tests
`backend/tests/test_heygen_routes.py` needs updating for new endpoint URL/format.

## Sandbox mode
LiveAvatar supports sandbox mode (free, 1-min sessions) by passing `"is_sandbox": true` in the token request. This is useful for development.

## Project Conventions
- Inline styles (no CSS framework)
- Frontend: TypeScript strict mode
- Backend: Python 3.11+ / FastAPI
- Socket.io events: snake_case
- Existing tests: Vitest (frontend), pytest (backend)

## Plan Requirements
1. Rewrite `useHeyGenAvatar.ts` → `useLiveAvatar.ts` using new SDK
2. Update backend token endpoint for LiveAvatar API
3. Re-integrate avatar + session lifecycle + voice + products into mirror page
4. Add Start Session button (from earlier plan)
5. Update package.json (remove old SDK, add new)
6. Update backend tests
7. Keep the same public hook interface where possible so mirror page integration is clean

Design a concrete, step-by-step implementation plan.
